type="text/javascript">\nP.when("A").register("DynamicIframe", function(A){\n\nfunction DynamicIframe(options) {\n  \n  var nTries = 0,\n      MAX_TRIES = 20,\n      iframeId = options.iframeId,\n      encodedIframeContent = options.encodedIframeContent,\n      iframeWrapperId = options.iframeWrapperId,\n      initialResizeCallback = options.initialResizeCallback,\n      iframeOverriddenCSSId = options.overriddenCSSId;\n    \n  this.createIframe = function() {\n    var iframe = document.createElement(\'iframe\');\n    var iframeCSS = document.getElementById(\'dynamic_iframe_CSS\').innerHTML;\n    var dir = \'auto\';\n\n    iframe.id = iframeId;\n    iframe.className = "ap_never_hide";\n    iframe.width = "100%";\n    iframe.scrolling = "no";\n    iframe.frameBorder = "0";\n    onloadFn = this.iframeload;\n    \n    if ( iframe.addEventListener )\n      iframe.addEventListener("load", onloadFn, false);\n    else if ( iframe.attachEvent )\n      iframe.attachEvent("onload", onloadFn);\n    else iframe.onload = onloadFn;\n    \n    var wrapper = document.getElementById(iframeWrapperId);\n    wrapper.innerHTML = \'\';\n    iframe = wrapper.appendChild(iframe);\n    \n    var overriddenCSS = "";\n    if (document.getElementById(iframeOverriddenCSSId)) {\n      overriddenCSS = document.getElementById(iframeOverriddenCSSId).innerHTML;\n    }\n\n    if (A.capabilities.rtl) {\n\n      var mapObj = {\n      left:\'right\',\n      right:\'left\',\n      };\n\n      iframeCSS = iframeCSS.replace(/left|right/gi, function(matched){\n        return mapObj[matched];\n      });\n\n      dir = \'rtl\';\n    }\n   \n    var doc = ((iframe.contentWindow && iframe.contentWindow.document) || iframe.contentDocument);\n    if (doc && doc.open) {\n      doc.open();\n\n      doc.writeln(\'<html><head><base target="_top" /></\' + \'head><body><style class="text/css">\' + iframeCSS + overriddenCSS + \'</style><div id="iframeContent" dir="\'+dir+\'">\' + \n              \'\' + decodeURIComponent(encodedIframeContent) + \'</\'+\'div>\'+\'</\'+\'body></html>\');\n      doc.close();\n    }\n  }\n  \n  this.iframeload = function () {\n    var iframe = document.getElementById(iframeId);\n    iframe.style.display = \'\';\n    setTimeout(function () {\n      setIframeHeight(initialResizeCallback);\n    }, 20); \n  }\n  \n  function getDocHeight(doc) {\n    var contentDiv = doc.getElementById("iframeContent");\n    var docHeight = 0;\n    if(contentDiv){\n      docHeight = Math.max(\n        contentDiv.scrollHeight,\n        contentDiv.offsetHeight,\n        contentDiv.clientHeight\n      );\n    }\n    return docHeight;\n  }\n  \n  function setIframeHeight(resizeCallback) {\n    var iframeDoc, iframe = document.getElementById(iframeId);\n    iframeDoc = ((iframe.contentWindow && iframe.contentWindow.document) || iframe.contentDocument);\n    if (iframeDoc) {\n      var h = getDocHeight(iframeDoc);\n      if (h && h != 0) {\n        iframe.style.height = parseInt(h) + \'px\';\n        if(typeof resizeCallback == "function") {\n          resizeCallback(iframeId); \n        }\n      } else if (nTries < MAX_TRIES) {\n        nTries++;\n        setTimeout(function () {\n            setIframeHeight(resizeCallback);\n        }, 50);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  \n  this.resizeIframe = function(resizeCallback) {\n    nTries = 0;\n  setIframeHeight(resizeCallback);\n  }\n}\n\nreturn DynamicIframe;\n});\n\x3C/script>                             </div>\n                                <div id="twisterPlusWWDesktop" class="celwidget" A companion to the #1 New York Times bestseller The Official Harry Potter Baking Book and The Official Harry Potter Cookbook!  </span> </td>          <td class="apm-tablemodule-valuecell" style="width:191px;"> <span class="a-size-base"> Ever wanted to fold your own Howler? Experience the Wizarding World like never before with this deluxe, 112-page origami guidebook!  </span> </td>     </tr>\n                 </tbody>\n            </table>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n</div>\n<div style="clear:both"></div>\n    </div>\n\n        </div>\n </div>\n       </div>                               </div>\n                                <div id="legal_feature_div" class="celwidget" data-feature-name="legal" data-csa-c-type="widget" data-csa-c-content-id="legal" data-csa-c-slot-id="legal_feature_div" data-csa-c-asin="" data-csa-c-is-in-initial-active-row="false" data-csa-c-id="nenf62-4ii766-u0h1lb-3rtb8c" data-cel-widget="legal_feature_div">\n                                                           </div>\n                                <div id="editorialReviews_feature_div" class="celwidget" data-feature-name="editorialReviews" data-csa-c-type="widget" data-csa-c-content-id="editorialReviews" data-csa-c-slot-id="editorialReviews_feature_div" data-csa-c-asin="" data-csa-c-is-in-initial-active-row="false" data-csa-c-id="7rhz3q-9973bn-41zlh-kwvtws" data-cel-widget="editorialReviews_feature_div">\n                                   <div class="a-divider a-divider-section"><div class="a-divider-inner"></div></div>     <h2> Editorial Reviews </h2>        <div class="a-section a-spacing-small a-padding-base">          <h3><span>About the Author</span></h3>  <div class="a-section a-spacing-small a-padding-small"> <p><span>J.K. Rowling is the author of the enduringly popular Harry Potter books. After the idea for Harry Potter came to her on a delayed train journey in 1990, she plotted out and started writing the series of seven books and the first was published as </span><span class="a-text-italic">Harry Potter and the Philosopher’s Stone</span><span> in the UK in 1997. The series took another ten years to complete, concluding in 2007 with the publication of </span><span class="a-text-italic">Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows</span><span>. </span></p><p></p><p><span>To accompany the series, J.K. Rowling wrote three short companion volumes for charity, </span><span class="a-text-italic">Quidditch Through the Ages</span><span>and </span><span class="a-text-italic">Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them</span><span>, in aid of Comic Relief and Lumos, and </span><span class="a-text-italic">The Tales of Beedle the Bard</span><span>, in aid of Lumos. She also collaborated on the writing of a stage play, </span><span class="a-text-italic">Harry Potter and the Cursed Child</span><span>, which was published as a script book.</span></p><p></p><p><span>Her other books for children include the fairy tale </span><span class="a-text-italic">The Ickabog</span><span> and </span><span class="a-text-italic">The Christmas Pig</span><span>, which were published in 2020 and 2021 respectively and have also been bestsellers. She is also the author of books for adults, including a bestselling crime fiction series.</span></p><p></p><p><span>J.K. Rowling has received many awards and honors for her writing. She also supports a number of causes through her charitable trust Volant and is the founder of the children’s charity Lumos.</span></p><p></p><p><span>To find out more about J.K. Rowling visit jkrowlingstories.com.</span></p><span><br> Karl James Mountford is an illustrator who works in both traditional and digital media. Karl specializes in book cover illustrations and also creates picture books, with his recent authorial titles including </span><span class="a-text-italic">The Circles in the Sky</span><span> and </span><span class="a-text-italic">A Voice in the Storm</span><span>. He currently lives and works in Wales, United Kingdom, with his dog, chickens, and any other wildlife that wants to stay. <br> Jess Tice-Gilbert is an artist, paper engineer, and graphic designer. She has been creating pop-ups for over twenty years and has worked in the studios of renowned paper engineers Robert Sabuda and Matthew Reinhart. Jess has designed and engineered pop-ups for the Museum of Modern Art, Chanel, Google, HBO, Papyrus, and Lucasfilm, among others. She currently is based in California.</span>  </div>      </div>                                   </div>\n                                <div id="detailBullets_feature_div" class="celwidget" href="/J-K-Rowling/e/B000AP9A6K/ref=aufs_dp_mata_dsk"><h2>J.K. Rowling</h2></a></div><div class="a-row a-spacing-base a-spacing-top-medium a-grid-vertical-align a-grid-center"><div class="a-cardui-deck" data-a-remove-top-gutter="true" data-a-remove-bottom-gutter="true" name="a-cardui-deck-autoname-1"><div class="a-teaser-describedby-collapsed a-hidden" id="a-cardui-deck-autoname-1-teaser-describedby-collapsed">Brief content visible, double tap to read full content.</div><div class="a-teaser-describedby-expanded a-hidden" id="a-cardui-deck-autoname-1-teaser-describedby-expanded">Full content visible, double tap to read brief content.</div><div class="a-cardui _about-the-author-card_carouselItemStyles_expander__3Fm-M" data-a-card-type="peekExpand" name="a-cardui-deck-autoname-1-card0"><div class="a-cardui-body"><div class="a-reactive-container a-reactive-container-transition" style="height: 80px;"><div><div class="a-cardui-content a-cardui-uninitialized _about-the-author-card_carouselItemStyles_peekableContent__hWM3i" data-a-max-height="80" style="max-height:80px"><p>J.K. Rowling is the author of the enduringly popular, era-defining Harry Potter book series, as well as several stand-alone novels for adults and children, and a bestselling crime fiction series written under the pen name Robert Galbraith.</p><p>The Harry Potter books have now sold over 600 million copies worldwide, been translated into 85 languages and made into eight blockbuster films. They continue to be discovered and loved by new generations of readers. A new faithful TV adaptation of the Harry Potter books is currently in production, and full-cast editions of the audiobooks are now out. </p><p>To accompany the series, J.K. Rowling wrote three short companion volumes for charity: Quidditch Through the Ages and Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them, in aid of Comic Relief, and The Tales of Beedle the Bard, in aid of her international children’s charity, Lumos. She also collaborated on the writing of a stage play, Harry Potter and the Cursed Child, which was published as a script book. </p><p>Her other books for younger children include The Ickabog and The Christmas Pig, which were published in 2020 and 2021 respectively and have been global bestsellers.</p><p>J.K. Rowling also writes novels for adults. The Casual Vacancy was published in 2012 and adapted for television in 2015. Under the pseudonym Robert Galbraith, she is the author of the highly acclaimed ‘Strike’ crime series, featuring private detectives Cormoran Strike and Robin Ellacott. </p><p>J.K. Rowling has received many awards and honours for her writing. She also supports a number of humanitarian causes through her charitable trust, Volant and is the founder of the children\'s charity Lumos.</p><p>Image: © J.K. Rowling</p></div></div><div class="a-reactive-container-gradient"></div></div></div><div aria-hidden="true" class="a-cardui-footer"><div class="a-cardui-expand-control-footer" data-csa-c-type="widget" customer reviews and ratings work</span></a><div data-expanded="false" class="a-expander-content a-expander-inline-content a-expander-inner" style="display:none"><p class="a-spacing-top-small">Customer Reviews, including Product Star Ratings help customers to learn more about the product and decide whether it is the right product for them.</p><p>To calculate the overall star rating and percentage breakdown by star, we don’t use a simple average. Instead, our system considers things like how recent a review is and if the reviewer bought the item on Amazon. It also analyzed reviews to verify trustworthiness.</p><a class="a-link-normal" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="_Y3Itb_cr-sidepanel-title_EX93d _Y3Itb_cr-sidepanel-truncated-title_2w0Qj">AMAZING CONTINUATION!!!</h5><div lang="en-US" class="_Y3Itb_cr-sidepanel-text-with-gradient_3T4-j"><div data-reviewbind="ReviewText" class="_Y3Itb_cr-sidepanel-text_3MYCz _Y3Itb_cr-sidepanel-truncated-text_3nfmo">AMAZING CONTINUATION!!!! I am a huge Harry Potter fan (I’ve read the entire series 4.5 times now) and I absolutely love the magic inspired my MiraLima. I don’t know why they didn’t continue their adaptation of the novels, BUT I do appreciate this continuation SO much. It came relatively shortly after the MinaLima contract ended, which didn’t leave me waiting too long. They continued most of the stylistic choices which I adore, because this book looks phenomenal next to the preceding 3. I absolutely love it. I love knowing (hoping?) this creator continues with books 5-7! There is so much power and beauty in those books, and I really hope Karl Mountford can continue his BEAUTIFUL work. Thank you Karl! I would’ve been heartbroken if someone hadn’t continued the beautiful journey that MinaLima had started.</div><div class="_Y3Itb_cr-sidepanel-container-gradient_1zm2j \'ready\').execute(function(A) {\n                if(typeof A.toggleExpanderAriaLabel === \'function\') {\n                    A.toggleExpanderAriaLabel(\'review_text_read_more\', \'Read more of this data-a-expander-collapsed-height="300" class="a-expander-collapsed-height a-row a-expander-container a-expander-partial-collapse-container review-text-read-more-expander" style="max-height: none; 24px;">\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n  \n  \n    <span>Like many, I was sorely disappointed to find out Mina Lima wasn’t designing any more of these. I had already bought the first 3 by that point. Then I found out they were going to finish the set after all, using a different artist. I was understandably skeptical that they would turn out well.<br><br>While the art inside &amp; out is certainly a different style, I still find it to be stunning. The spines don’t match exactly, but are similar enough to not be noticeably “different art”. In fact, I’m getting excited again about the prospect of having all the books done this way, different artists or not. The photo shows how they look together. They kept the number progression up the towers, which I love &amp; enough elements like the gold foil, the intricate framework &amp; detailed embossing that I think it fits in fine. Shelved together, it will still be an impressive collection.<br><br>I’ve already found many of the interactive pages to be clever and beautiful, as are the regular pages of the story. It still feels like reading an ornate “illuminated” tome. I’ve been obsessed with illuminated manuscripts for ages &amp; now it feels like I have my very own- and it’s one of my favorite stories of all time. Book 4 is a win!<br></span>\n  \n</div><div class="a-expander-header a-expander-partial-collapse-header" style="opacity: 1; display: block;"><div class="a-expander-content-fade"></div><a </div>\n\n            <i class="a-icon a-icon-star a-star-5 cr-lightbox-review-rating"><span class="a-icon-alt">5.0 out of 5 stars</span></i>\n            <h5 class="cr-lightbox-review-title">\n                Stunning addition, renewed hope for the series.\n            </h5>\n            <br>\n            \n                <span class="a-size-small a-color-secondary cr-lightbox-review-origin">\n                    \n                        \n                        \n                            \n                                \n                                \n                                    Reviewed in the United States on October 14, 2025\n                                \n                            \n                        \n                    \n                </span>\n            \n        </div>\n        <span class="a-size-base cr-lightbox-review-body">\n            Like many, I was sorely disappointed to find out Mina Lima wasn’t designing any more of these. I had already bought the first 3 by that point. Then I found out they were going to finish the set after all, using a different artist. I was understandably skeptical that they would turn out well.<br><br>While the art inside &amp; out is certainly a different style, I still find it to be stunning. The spines don’t match exactly, but are similar enough to not be noticeably “different art”. In fact, I’m getting excited again about the prospect of having all the books done this way, different artists or not. The photo shows how they look together. They kept the number progression up the towers, which I love &amp; enough elements like the gold foil, the intricate framework &amp; detailed embossing that I think it fits in fine. Shelved together, it will still be an impressive collection.<br><br>I’ve already found many of the interactive pages to be clever and beautiful, as are the regular pages of the story. It still feels like reading an ornate “illuminated” tome. I’ve been obsessed with illuminated manuscripts for ages &amp; now it feels like I have my very own- and it’s one of my favorite stories of all time. Book 4 is a win!<br>\n        </span>\n        <div id="cr-lightbox-image-picker" class="a-section a-spacing-top-base">\n            <h6 id="arp-images-in-review" class="a-size-medium class="a-profile-name">Kaya</span></div></a></div><div class="a-row"><h5><a data-hook="review-title" class="a-size-base a-link-normal review-title a-color-base review-title-content a-text-bold" href="/gp/customer-reviews/R1P8ZWVXU3ZFRS/ref=cm_cr_dp_d_rvw_ttl?ie=UTF8"><i data-hook="review-star-rating" class="a-icon a-icon-star a-star-4 review-rating"><span class="a-icon-alt">4.0 out of 5 stars</span></i><span class="a-letter-space"></span>\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n  \n  \n    <span>Beautiful, but Missing the Magic of the First Three</span>\n  \n</a></h5></div><span data-hook="review-date" aria-level="6" class="a-size-base a-color-secondary review-date" role="heading">Reviewed in the United States on October 14, 2025</span><div class="a-row a-spacing-mini review-data review-format-strip"><span data-hook="format-strip-linkless" class="a-color-secondary">Format: Hardcover</span><i aria-hidden="true" class="a-icon a-icon-text-separator" role="img" aria-label="|"></i><a aria-label="Verified Purchase: Learn more how customers reviews work on Amazon" class="a-link-normal" target="_blank" rel="noopener" href="/gp/help/customer/display.html/ref=cm_cr_dp_d_rvw_avp?nodeId=G8UYX7LALQC8V9KA"><span data-hook="avp-badge-linkless" class="a-size-mini a-color-state a-text-bold">Verified Purchase</span></a></div><div class="a-row a-spacing-small review-data"><span data-hook="review-body" class="a-size-base review-text"><div data-a-expander-name="review_text_read_more" data-a-expander-collapsed-height="300" class="a-expander-collapsed-height a-row a-expander-container a-expander-partial-collapse-container review-text-read-more-expander" style="max-height: none; height: 300px;"><div data-hook="review-collapsed" data-expanded="false" class="a-expander-content reviewText review-text-content a-expander-partial-collapse-content" style="padding-bottom: 24px;">\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n  \n  \n    <span>It’s not quite as good as the first three books that MinaLima worked on. Don’t get me wrong, it’s still a beautiful book with lovely illustrations. But it just doesn’t hit the same way the others did.<br><br>Let’s start with the illustrations. They’re gorgeous, and Carl James Mountford did a solid job capturing MinaLima’s style. The problem is with the ideas behind them. They feel a little uninspired, like there wasn’t much thought put into the concepts. There’s no real “wow” moment.<br><br>Another thing I noticed is that there aren’t nearly as many illustrations as in the earlier books. I get that this one’s longer, but there are huge sections with no artwork at all, which makes it feel less magical.<br><br>And finally, the interactive pages are disappointing. They’re not as creative or sturdy as before… they feel flimsy, like they could tear easily. Overall, it’s still a nice book, just not on the same level as the first three.<br></span>\n  \n</div><div class="a-expander-header a-expander-partial-collapse-header" style="opacity: 1; display: block;"><div class="a-expander-content-fade"></div><a href="javascript:void(0)" data-hook="expand-collapse-read-more-less" aria-label="Read more of this review" aria-expanded="false" role="button" &quot;collapse_prompt&quot;:&quot;Read less&quot;}"><i class="a-icon a-icon-extender-expand"></i><span class="a-expander-prompt">Read more</span></a></div></div></span></div><div illustrated, interactive editions of the books. I was worried when I heard MinaLima (the illustrators who did the movies and the first three books in this style) would not be finishing the series and that another illustrator was taking over; however; I am very pleasantly surprised with this fourth book! It looks great and has the same look and feel as the first three. They look fantastic as a set on my shelf! These would make great gifts for any HP fan in your life, but I’d recommend having an adult help young kids with them so they don’t get destroyed. They really bring the magic of the stories to life, so they would be fun to read to kids though!<br></span>\n  \n</div><div class="a-expander-header a-expander-partial-collapse-header" style="opacity: review-data review-format-strip"><span data-hook="format-strip-linkless" class="a-color-secondary">Format: Hardcover</span><i aria-hidden="true" class="a-icon a-icon-text-separator" role="img" aria-label="|"></i><a aria-label="Verified Purchase: Learn more how customers reviews work on Amazon" class="a-link-normal" target="_blank" rel="noopener" href="/gp/help/customer/display.html/ref=cm_cr_dp_d_rvw_avp?nodeId=G8UYX7LALQC8V9KA"><span data-hook="avp-badge-linkless" class="a-size-mini a-color-state a-text-bold">Verified Purchase</span></a></div><div class="a-row a-spacing-small review-data"><span data-hook="review-body" class="a-size-base review-text"><div data-a-expander-name="review_text_read_more" data-a-expander-collapsed-height="300" class="a-expander-collapsed-height a-row a-expander-container a-expander-partial-collapse-container review-text-read-more-expander" style="max-height:300px"><div data-hook="review-collapsed" data-expanded="false" class="a-expander-content reviewText review-text-content a-expander-partial-collapse-content">\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n  \n  \n    <span>AMAZING CONTINUATION!!!! I am a huge Harry Potter fan (I’ve read the entire series 4.5 times now) and I absolutely love the magic inspired my MiraLima. I don’t know why they didn’t continue their adaptation of the novels, BUT I do appreciate this continuation SO much. It came relatively shortly after the MinaLima contract ended, which didn’t leave me waiting too long. They continued most of the stylistic choices which I adore, because this book looks phenomenal next to the preceding 3. I absolutely love it. I love knowing (hoping?) this creator continues with books 5-7! There is so much power and beauty in those books, and I really hope Karl Mountford can continue his BEAUTIFUL work. Thank you Karl! I would’ve been heartbroken if someone hadn’t continued the beautiful journey that MinaLima had started.<br></span>\n  \n</div><div class="a-expander-header a-expander-partial-collapse-header"      </div>\n\n            <i class="a-icon a-icon-star a-star-5 cr-lightbox-review-rating"><span class="a-icon-alt">5.0 out of 5 stars</span></i>\n            <h5 class="cr-lightbox-review-title">\n                AMAZING CONTINUATION!!!\n            </h5>\n            <br>\n            \n                <span class="a-size-small a-color-secondary cr-lightbox-review-origin">\n                    \n                        \n                        \n                            \n                                \n                                \n                                    Reviewed in the United States on October 24, 2025\n                                \n                            \n                        \n                    \n                </span>\n            \n        </div>\n        <span class="a-size-base cr-lightbox-review-body">\n            AMAZING CONTINUATION!!!! I am a huge Harry Potter fan (I’ve read the entire series 4.5 times now) and I absolutely love the magic inspired my MiraLima. I don’t know why they didn’t continue their adaptation of the novels, BUT I do appreciate this continuation SO much. It came relatively shortly after the MinaLima contract ended, which didn’t leave me waiting too long. They continued most of the stylistic choices which I adore, because this book looks phenomenal next to the preceding 3. I absolutely love it. I love knowing (hoping?) this creator continues with books 5-7! There is so much power and beauty in those books, and I really hope Karl Mountford can continue his BEAUTIFUL work. Thank you Karl! I would’ve been heartbroken if someone hadn’t continued the beautiful journey that MinaLima had started.<br>\n        </span>\n        <div id="cr-lightbox-image-picker" class="a-section a-spacing-top-base">\n            <h6 id="arp-images-in-review" class="a-size-medium a-color-secondary cr-lightbox-images-in-review-title a-text-normal">\n                Images in this review\n class="cr-original-review-content" lang="es">Mágico, cumplió con todo lo esperado para ser el cuarto libro. Me preocupaba un poco el cambio de las ilustraciones y el arte por el cambio de minalima, pero la verdad que lo hicieron genial. Hermoso trabajo.<br></span><span class="cr-translated-review-content aok-hidden"></span>\n  \n  \n</div><div class="a-expander-header a-expander-partial-collapse-header" style="opacity: 0; display:
